message:
feat(harness): add no-flicker invariant under all fault scenarios (bd-1pys5.5)

30 test scenarios verify the no-flicker invariant holds under every
fault injection category:
- Frame drops: single, burst, periodic, random (5 tests)
- Resize thrash: oscillation, shrink/grow, random sizes, extremes (4 tests)
- Bursty input: full rewrite, alternating, high churn, sparse, styled (5 tests)
- Capability mismatch: sync on/off, truecolor, mid-session switch (4 tests)
- Combined faults: drops+resize, bursty+drops, all three, caps+resize+drops (4 tests)
- Stress: 200 frames mixed faults, 100 frames periodic drops (2 tests)
- Edge cases: empty buffer, 1x1, no-change, wide/tall buffers (5 tests)
- Determinism: same inputs produce identical flicker analysis (1 test)

Key invariant: with sync_output enabled, every frame has complete
?2026h/?2026l brackets and zero visible content outside brackets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1pys5.2","title":"task(fault-injection): Resize thrash (rapid resize events)","description":"## Summary

Implement fault injection for rapid terminal resize events. Resize is the most disruptive event in a TUI — it invalidates all layout, requires full reflow, and can expose race conditions between input processing and rendering.

## Detail

Resize thrash scenarios:
1. **Rapid oscillation**: Toggle between 80x24 and 120x40 every 16ms (60Hz)
2. **Progressive shrink**: Decrease terminal size by 1 column/row every 16ms until 10x5
3. **Progressive grow**: Increase terminal size by 1 column/row every 16ms until 300x100
4. **Random sizes**: Random terminal size every 16ms for 10 seconds
5. **Extreme sizes**: Resize to 1x1, then to 1000x1000, then to 10x10000

Expected behavior:
- No panic or crash at any terminal size
- Layout recomputes correctly at every size (verified by golden frame checksums)
- Frame render time stays bounded (no quadratic blowup on extreme sizes)
- Widget content is clipped correctly, not rendered out of bounds
- Scroll state is preserved across resizes (proportional scrolling)
- No ANSI escape sequence corruption (complete sequences emitted per resize)

Critical invariant: After resize thrash, returning to original size produces identical output to never having resized. This is the "resize round-trip" property.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 5 resize scenarios implemented
2. No panics or crashes at any terminal size
3. Resize round-trip property verified (return to original size = identical output)
4. Frame time bounded by O(W*H) — no quadratic behavior
5. ANSI escape sequences never corrupted (validated by terminal emulator parser)
6. Results recorded in structured JSONL for evidence","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:26.443286950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T07:33:49.635709617Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T07:33:49.635674291Z","close_reason":"Completed resize-thrash fault-injection implementation and validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fault-injection","graveyard","resize","thrash"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1pys5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:26.443286950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pys5.3","title":"task(fault-injection): Terminal capability mismatch","description":"## Summary

Implement fault injection for terminal capability mismatches — situations where FrankenTUI assumes capabilities the terminal doesn't support (e.g., 256-color on a 16-color terminal, mouse capture on a terminal without mouse support).

## Detail

Capability mismatch scenarios:
1. **Color downgrade**: Request 24-bit color on 16-color terminal
2. **No mouse**: Enable mouse capture on terminal that ignores mouse escapes
3. **No alternate screen**: Enter alternate screen on terminal that doesn't support it
4. **No Unicode**: Emit wide characters on terminal with ASCII-only support
5. **Slow terminal**: Terminal that takes 50ms+ to process each escape sequence
6. **Broken bracketed paste**: Terminal that sends paste content without bracketing

Expected behavior:
- Graceful degradation: fall back to closest supported capability
- Color quantization: 24-bit → 256 → 16 → 8 automatically
- Feature detection: probe terminal capabilities at startup
- No garbled output: if a feature isn't supported, don't emit its escape sequences
- Warning log: record capability mismatches for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 6 mismatch scenarios implemented as test cases
2. Graceful degradation verified (output is usable, not garbled)
3. Color quantization produces visually acceptable results
4. No escape sequence pollution (unsupported escapes not emitted)
5. Capability detection documented and tested","notes":"Claimed by FoggyPeak; implementing terminal capability mismatch fault-injection scenarios and evidence checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:36.801359511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T07:45:49.381501817Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T07:45:49.381481870Z","close_reason":"Implemented and validated terminal capability mismatch fault-injection matrix","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capability","fault-injection","graveyard","mismatch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1pys5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:36.801359511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pys5.4","title":"task(fault-injection): Forced frame drops","description":"## Summary

Implement fault injection for forced frame drops — simulate situations where frames are skipped due to computation taking longer than the frame budget. Verify that FrankenTUI recovers gracefully and the next frame is correct.

## Detail

Frame drop scenarios:
1. **Single frame drop**: Skip one frame, verify next frame is correct
2. **Burst frame drops**: Drop 5 consecutive frames, verify recovery
3. **Periodic frame drops**: Drop every 3rd frame for 10 seconds
4. **Layout timeout**: Inject 50ms delay in layout computation, causing frame budget overrun
5. **Diff timeout**: Inject 50ms delay in diff computation

Expected behavior:
- After frame drop, the next rendered frame is fully correct (not a partial update)
- No accumulated visual artifacts from dropped frames
- Input events during dropped frames are not lost (queued and processed)
- Frame counter accounts for dropped frames (telemetry shows gap)
- The diff engine does not assume the previous frame is the one that was displayed (it may have been dropped)

Critical invariant: Frame N+2 after dropping frame N+1 must be identical to what frame N+2 would have been WITHOUT the drop. Frame drops are invisible to the visual output — only frame timing is affected.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 5 frame drop scenarios implemented
2. Post-drop frames are pixel-identical to no-drop baseline (verified by checksum)
3. No accumulated visual artifacts
4. Input events preserved during drops
5. Telemetry correctly records dropped frames","notes":"Claimed by FoggyPeak; starting forced frame-drop fault-injection harness work from PTY layer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:47.753707149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T15:05:12.217246963Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T15:05:12.217219982Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fault-injection","frame-drop","graveyard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1pys5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:47.753707149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1230,"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TanLion: Implemented 37 test scenarios covering all 5 frame drop categories: single drop (7 tests), burst drops (5 tests), periodic drops (5 tests), layout/diff delay (4 tests), post-drop correctness (2 tests), edge cases (7 tests), stateful widgets (2 tests), stress tests (5 tests). All pass, clippy clean. Key invariant verified: frame N+2 after dropping N+1 produces identical buffer checksums as presenting N, N+2 directly.","created_at":"2026-02-16T15:05:07Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1pys5.5","title":"task(e2e): No-flicker invariant under all fault scenarios","description":"## Summary

End-to-end test that verifies the no-flicker invariant: under ALL fault injection scenarios, the user never sees a partially-rendered or corrupted frame. This is the ultimate quality gate.

## Detail

No-flicker definition:
- Every ANSI escape sequence sent to the terminal is complete and valid
- No cursor jump to a position and then jump away without writing (visible cursor flicker)
- No cell is written twice in a single frame (double-render flicker)
- The diff engine never emits a diff that would cause visible flicker (e.g., clear-then-redraw)
- Hidden cursor during render, restored after

Test methodology:
1. Wrap the terminal output in a capture layer that records all bytes written
2. Parse the capture as ANSI escape sequences
3. Verify invariants:
   a. Every CSI sequence is complete (no truncation)
   b. Cursor is hidden at start of render, shown at end
   c. No cell is written more than once per frame
   d. No clear-screen followed by full redraw (should be incremental)
   e. Cursor position is restored to expected location after render

Run this verification under each fault scenario:
- Bursty input storms
- Resize thrash
- Capability mismatch
- Forced frame drops
- Combined: multiple faults simultaneously

## Acceptance Criteria

1. No-flicker invariant holds under all individual fault scenarios
2. No-flicker invariant holds under combined fault scenarios
3. ANSI sequence validation catches all truncation/corruption
4. Test produces clear failure output showing the exact flicker event
5. CI-gated: any flicker detection fails the build","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:01.142030013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:34.872218213Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","graveyard","invariant","no-flicker"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1pys5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:01.142030013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1pys5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:33.618473684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1pys5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:34.039517151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1pys5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:34.469304057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1pys5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:34.872171115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1pys5.6","title":"task(e2e): Deterministic frame hash parity across 100 scenarios","description":"## Summary

End-to-end test verifying that frame hashes are deterministic and reproducible across 100+ test scenarios. Given the same input sequence, the same frame buffer must be produced every time, regardless of timing, system load, or environmental factors.

## Detail

Determinism sources to control:
- Random number generators: must be seeded deterministically
- Timing: frame timestamps must be injectable (not wall-clock dependent)
- System state: terminal size, capabilities must be mocked
- Input order: events must be processed in a defined order

Test methodology:
1. Define 100+ test scenarios with explicit:
   - Initial terminal size
   - Widget tree definition
   - Input event sequence with logical timestamps
   - Expected frame hashes at each checkpoint
2. Run each scenario in the deterministic simulator (Ring 2)
3. Compare frame hashes against golden values
4. Any mismatch = test failure with detailed diff

Scenario categories (aim for 100+ total):
- Simple widgets: text, input, button, list (20 scenarios)
- Complex layouts: flex, grid, split, tabs (20 scenarios)
- Interaction sequences: typing, scrolling, focus, resize (30 scenarios)
- Edge cases: empty, oversized, Unicode, RTL (20 scenarios)
- Regression scenarios: previously-found bugs (10+ scenarios)

## Acceptance Criteria

1. 100+ scenarios defined and passing
2. All frame hashes reproducible across CI runs
3. Scenario definitions are human-readable (TOML or similar)
4. Adding a new scenario is straightforward (template provided)
5. Failures produce visual diffs showing frame difference","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:14.034474477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T14:33:45.941450251Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T14:33:45.941425866Z","close_reason":"Implemented 103 test scenarios across 5 categories: simple widgets (20), complex layouts (20), interaction sequences (30), edge cases (20), regression scenarios (12+1 summary). All pass deterministically.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic","e2e","frame-hash","graveyard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1pys5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:14.034474477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3jlw5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:01.325267345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pys5.7","title":"task(e2e): 60fps sustained on 1000-widget tree for 60 seconds","description":"## Summary

Soak test: verify that FrankenTUI sustains 60fps rendering on a 1000-widget tree for 60 continuous seconds with active interaction (scrolling, typing, focus changes). This validates that there are no slow leaks, GC-like pauses, or progressive degradation.

## Detail

Test setup:
- Widget tree: 1000 widgets (mix of text, list, input, flex containers)
- Interaction: automated input sequence simulating active user (typing + scrolling + tab)
- Frame budget: 16.67ms (60fps)
- Duration: 60 seconds (3600 frames)

Pass criteria:
- p50 frame time < 8ms (50% headroom)
- p95 frame time < 14ms (16% headroom)
- p99 frame time < 16ms (4% headroom)
- MAX frame time < 33ms (never drop below 30fps)
- Zero frames with visual corruption (verified by checksum stability)
- Memory usage: stable or decreasing over time (no leaks)
- Allocation count: stable per frame (no accumulation)

Measurements:
- Frame time histogram (1ms buckets)
- Memory RSS sampled every second
- Allocation count per frame (via custom allocator)
- Frame checksum per frame (for corruption detection)
- Event queue depth per frame (for backpressure detection)

Failure analysis (if soak fails):
- Identify the frame where regression starts
- Capture flamegraph of that frame
- Diff against early-session flamegraph
- Report: memory growth rate, allocation accumulation rate

## Acceptance Criteria

1. 60fps sustained for full 60 seconds (pass criteria above)
2. Memory stable: RSS at end - RSS at start < 1MB
3. Allocations stable: per-frame allocation count variance < 10%
4. No visual corruption: all frame checksums consistent with expected output
5. Results stored as CI artifacts (frame time histogram, memory trace, flamegraph)
6. Regression gate: any SLA violation fails CI","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:28.899496278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:28.899496278Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["60fps","e2e","graveyard","performance","soak"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pys5.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1pys5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:28.899496278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1q5","title":"Epic: Ring 1 Kernel - Terminal Lifecycle & Cell/Buffer/Frame","description":"Core terminal kernel: terminal lifecycle management, cell/buffer/frame model, diff engine, ANSI presenter. Foundation for all higher rings. Includes Bayesian diff strategy selection (Beta posterior), ANSI escape sequence state machine, sync bracket completeness proofs for flicker-free guarantee, and deterministic rendering pipeline.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:57:31.841282769Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:57:31.841282769Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","kernel","ring1"]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.7","title":"task(docs): publish advanced API migration/capability mapping and troubleshooting guide","description":"Background
- Adoption requires clear migration semantics for advanced APIs, not only base terminal operations.

Goal
- Produce a migration and capability mapping for advanced host APIs from xterm.js concepts to FrankenTermJS primitives.

Scope
- Map key xterm.js advanced APIs/events to FrankenTermJS equivalents and intentional divergences.
- Document capability flags, security constraints, and deterministic guarantees.
- Include troubleshooting guidance tied to structured logs from compatibility harness.

Why This Matters
- Reduces migration friction and support load while preserving intentional architectural differences.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Include doc-tests or schema checks ensuring documented API signatures match shipped types.
- Validate examples via E2E smoke scripts in CI.
- Ensure documentation references structured log fields used during debugging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Migration guide is complete for advanced API parity scope with explicit compatibility table.
- Examples compile/run in CI and match current API signatures.
- Logging/troubleshooting sections are actionable and aligned with implemented telemetry.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:47.059403237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:23:13.299539869Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","docs","frankenterm","migration","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:47.059403237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:14.125633722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): addon-parity closure (fit, web-links, image, progress, ligatures, web-fonts)","description":"Background\n- xterm.js adoption in production often depends on addon capabilities beyond the base terminal surface.\n- Current FrankenTermJS planning covers core remote mode, scrollback/search, rendering, SDK, and conformance, but does not explicitly track parity for fit/web-links/image/progress/ligatures/web-fonts as first-class deliverables.\n\nGoal\n- Close the remaining addon-class capability gap with deterministic, first-party FrankenTermJS modules (not plugin baggage), while preserving bounded-resource and standards-first architecture.\n\nIn Scope\n- Fit-to-container semantics and robust resize observation (fit parity).\n- Hyperlink detection/provider APIs and secure activation UX (web-links parity).\n- Inline image protocol support with bounded decode/render policy (image parity).\n- Progress signaling/event API semantics (progress parity).\n- Optional ligature/shaping rendering path with deterministic fallback (ligatures parity).\n- Web-font lifecycle + metric invalidation controls (web-fonts parity).\n\nOut of Scope\n- Recreating xterm.js addon/plugin architecture as-is.\n- Unbounded buffers/memory for rich media or layout adaptation.\n\nWhy This Matters\n- This closes practical migration blockers for real-world xterm.js users and ensures FrankenTermJS can credibly be a complete replacement, not just a fast core renderer.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- All addon-parity capabilities in scope are implemented with explicit deterministic contracts and capability flags.\n- Unit tests cover core invariants and edge cases for each capability area (fit, links, image, progress, ligatures, web-font lifecycle).\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts cover representative user workflows and emit detailed structured logs (JSONL) with trace/correlation metadata.\n- CI release gates fail on regressions in addon-parity correctness, determinism, or bounded-resource constraints.\n- Migration/readiness artifacts explicitly map these capabilities to xterm.js parity scorecard entries.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this feature.\n- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for materially affected workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Treat each addon-parity capability as a first-party module behind a stable API; avoid plugin-system debt.\n- Ensure every capability has both correctness tests and stress/failure diagnostics with structured logs.\n- Prioritize deterministic behavior and bounded resource usage over opportunistic best-effort rendering.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:12.895368950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:19.255137851Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["addons","fit","frankenterm","image","ligatures","links","parity","progress","wasm","web-fonts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:12.895368950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","title":"task(layout): implement deterministic fit-to-container and web-font metric lifecycle","description":"Background\n- xterm.js users expect reliable fit behavior plus font-loading integration without cursor drift or geometry jumps.\n- FrankenTermJS currently lacks an explicit bead for this parity surface.\n\nScope\n- Implement deterministic fit-to-container API with host callbacks/resize observation contract.\n- Define font-load invalidation pipeline: metric recompute, cache invalidation, patch regeneration boundaries, and throttling.\n- Handle DPR/zoom/container resizes without off-by-one geometry drift.\n\nAcceptance\n- Equivalent resize/font-load event streams yield identical viewport + cursor geometry outcomes.\n- No unbounded resize queue growth under storm conditions.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Fit API behavior is deterministic for fixed input streams (resize, font-load, DPR changes).\n- Unit tests cover metric recompute ordering, cache invalidation, and queue-throttling edge cases.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate resize storms, zoom transitions, and delayed web-font loads with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- Geometry drift, cursor misalignment, and stale-glyph artifacts are regression-gated in CI.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep metric and fit logic in deterministic Rust/WASM state; JS host should remain thin.\n- Explicitly document rounding/tie-break policies for fractional geometry to avoid browser divergence.\n- Add failure diagnostics for font-load race windows and resize burst coalescing decisions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:27.908441965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T00:53:14.651803885Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T00:53:14.651785019Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fit","frankenterm","layout","renderer","wasm","web-fonts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:27.908441965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:44.295223338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:43.930817274Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","title":"task(links): implement OSC-8 + URL-provider hyperlink detection and annotation pipeline","description":"Background\n- xterm.js web-links usage depends on both OSC-8 hyperlinks and plain-text URL detection/provider behavior.\n- FrankenTermJS needs deterministic hyperlink discovery and annotation across viewport + scrollback.\n\nScope\n- Preserve OSC-8 hyperlink metadata through parser/state/grid/scrollback paths.\n- Implement deterministic URL detection/provider API with explicit ordering and conflict policy versus OSC-8.\n- Expose hyperlink annotation data for hover, keyboard navigation, and secure activation layers.\n\nAcceptance\n- Hyperlink ranges remain stable under scroll, resize, and reflow operations.\n- Detection conflicts resolve deterministically with documented precedence.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- OSC-8 and URL-provider detection semantics are deterministic and documented (precedence, overlap, invalidation).\n- Unit tests cover hyperlink range stability under unicode graphemes, wraps, scrollback trims, and viewport reflow.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate hover/focus annotation correctness with structured JSONL logs.\n- Differential checks versus xterm.js hyperlink fixtures are wired into CI for covered scenarios.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep link annotations in core deterministic state so selection/copy/search can consume the same canonical ranges.\n- Explicitly log overlap-resolution decisions (OSC-8 vs provider URL) for postmortem clarity.\n- Do not allow hyperlink metadata paths to introduce unbounded per-cell payload growth.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyGate","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:52.228283813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T15:03:23.287002632Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","links","parser","security","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:54:46.637014814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:04.522840714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:52.228283813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:04.879962033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:05.245751779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","title":"task(image): implement bounded inline-image protocol decoding and cell-anchored rendering","description":"Background\n- xterm.js image addon users rely on inline image protocols for modern terminal workflows.\n- FrankenTermJS needs first-party, policy-driven support with hard resource bounds.\n\nScope\n- Parse and validate selected inline image protocol envelopes (capability-gated).\n- Implement deterministic image decode/upload pipeline with strict memory/frame budgets and eviction policy.\n- Render cell-anchored images with correct scroll/resize/viewport behavior and fallback semantics when capabilities are unavailable.\n\nAcceptance\n- Image rendering remains deterministic for fixed inputs/capability profiles.\n- Decode/render paths enforce bounded memory and queue usage under adversarial input.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Protocol parsing/validation for supported inline-image formats is deterministic, capability-gated, and fuzz/regression tested.\n- Unit tests cover decode limits, eviction policy, anchor semantics, and resize/scroll interactions.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate image-heavy workloads and failure modes (oversized payloads, malformed metadata) with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- CI gates fail on budget regressions (memory/frame time) or semantic drift in image positioning behavior.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep all image protocol support explicitly capability-gated and policy-controlled by defaults.\n- Enforce hard decode/upload caps before allocation; fail deterministically with reason-coded diagnostics.\n- Ensure image rendering never bypasses one-writer/frame-budget invariants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:12.087634054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T05:36:52.374427753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T05:36:52.374343726Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","image","protocol","renderer","security","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:25.010551183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:12.087634054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:24.645156728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:25.371523095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","title":"task(progress): implement deterministic progress signaling API and OSC-mapped semantics","description":"Background\n- xterm.js progress addon usage depends on exposing terminal-originated progress signals to host UI.\n- FrankenTermJS should provide a first-party deterministic progress event surface without addon coupling.\n\nScope\n- Define supported progress signal sources (including mapped escape-sequence forms) and normalization rules.\n- Expose typed progress events with ordering guarantees and bounded delivery semantics.\n- Integrate with host API docs and capability flags.\n\nAcceptance\n- Equivalent input/replay streams produce identical progress event sequences.\n- Unsupported/prohibited signals fail deterministically with traceable diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Progress signal taxonomy and normalization rules are explicit, deterministic, and versioned.\n- Unit tests cover sequence ordering, dedupe/coalescing policy, invalid signal handling, and capability gating.\n- Browser E2E scripts validate host-visible progress lifecycle behavior with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- API contract tests ensure typed event payload stability and error semantics.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep progress events in the same deterministic ordering model as other host events.\n- Do not leak unbounded high-frequency progress updates; enforce bounded delivery/coalescing policy.\n- Include trace IDs linking parsed escape input to emitted host events.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:31.771885449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:54:02.228948627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","parser","progress","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:42.364874191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:42.787264441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:43.211698509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:31.771885449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","title":"task(text-render): implement optional ligature/shaping path with deterministic fallback","description":"Background\n- Many xterm.js users depend on ligature rendering for code readability in modern fonts.\n- FrankenTermJS should support this as an explicit, optional capability without sacrificing deterministic core behavior.\n\nScope\n- Add optional ligature/shaping render path with stable feature flags and fallback to canonical grapheme rendering.\n- Define cache/invalidation behavior for shaped clusters and font changes.\n- Ensure selection/cursor/extraction semantics remain correct when ligature shaping is enabled.\n\nAcceptance\n- Enabling/disabling ligature mode produces predictable, documented rendering differences only.\n- Core editing/navigation semantics remain invariant across ligature settings.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Ligature/shaping mode is capability-gated, deterministic, and documented with explicit fallback semantics.\n- Unit tests cover shaping cache invalidation, cursor/selection boundary correctness, and copy/extract invariants under ligature mode.\n- Browser E2E scripts validate ligature-enabled workflows across representative fonts with structured JSONL logs.\n- Performance and memory impacts are benchmarked and gated to prevent regressions.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Treat ligatures as a rendering concern; canonical text storage and terminal semantics must remain unchanged.\n- Document and test the exact mapping between shaped glyphs and logical cell boundaries for interaction correctness.\n- Ensure fallback path is always available and deterministic when shaping prerequisites are missing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:50.541282675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:54:02.598328717Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","ligatures","renderer","text","unicode","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:50.541282675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:01.684144315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:00.938379165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:01.315825142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
